description: Fix brainPlan state restore bugs (Bugs 1-2): the project switch dropdown does not restore uiState.brainPlan, and QIBoot does not restore it on page load. This causes Advisor/Frameworks/Executive Summary to show wrong or empty data after switching projects or reloading the page.
steps: In qi-webapp/js/ui.js, find the #projectSwitch change handler (around line 5995). The else branch calls S.switchProject(v); refreshHeader(); go(...) but does NOT restore brainPlan. Add `uiState.brainPlan = S.getBrainPlan() || null;` right after S.switchProject(v), matching what the openproj action does on line 5882.

In qi-webapp/js/ui.js, find window.QIBoot = function() (around line 6100). It calls S.load() but never restores brainPlan. Add `uiState.brainPlan = S.getBrainPlan() || null;` right after the S.load() call.

Add smoke test assertions to qi-webapp/smoke.js to verify: (a) After S.switchProject() the brainPlan is restored from the switched-to project, (b) After boot, brainPlan reflects the active project's stored plan.
acceptance_criteria: After switching projects via the dropdown, uiState.brainPlan reflects the switched-to project's stored plan (not the previous project's).
After page boot (QIBoot), uiState.brainPlan is populated from the active project's persisted plan.
New smoke assertions pass covering both scenarios.
All existing 963+ smoke tests continue to pass.
npm test and node verify-standalone.js both pass.
